name: Developer Room
landmark: Kabuki Market, inside the Prebab City shop
description: Head down the staircase inside the shop to a garage-style door. Enter the code 605185 on the keypad to get in. Sitting on the couch inside triggers a slideshow of the game's developers while Johnny plays guitar.

name: Blade Runner Rooftop Scene
landmark: Rooftop of the Advocet Hotel
description: A rooftop scene referencing the ending of Blade Runner, with an injured man holding a dove.

name: Vault Reference Site
landmark: Southern Badlands, an underground vault-style entrance surrounded by bodies
description: A group of LARPers met a violent end near a structure resembling a well-known vault entrance from another post-apocalyptic game series.

name: Edgerunners Memorial
landmark: The Columbarium, southern North Oak
description: Memorial plaques dedicated to characters from the Cyberpunk: Edgerunners anime.

name: Rayfield Caliburn
landmark: A hidden cave, found via a large storage container
description: One of the fastest vehicles in the game, tucked away with notes hinting at a masked vigilante. Reward tied to completing Panam's full questline.

name: Red/Blue Pill Scenes
landmark: Misty's shop (early game) and a later party scene while playing as Johnny
description: Two separate scenes referencing a famous pill-choice moment from a well-known sci-fi film.

name: Bathroom Seashells
landmark: On the shelf next to the toilet in V's bathroom
description: A trio of seashells referencing an old sci-fi comedy's take on future toilet habits.

name: Delamain AI Cameo
landmark: Encountered during the Epistrophy side quest line with Delamain
description: One of Delamain's defective taxi personalities is voiced by, and strongly resembles, a sarcastic AI character from a well-known puzzle game series.

name: Smuggler's Cache
landmark: Underwater near the bridge connecting Pacifica and Heywood
description: An underwater cache with a note referencing smuggler stashes from CD Projekt Red's previous fantasy RPG series.

name: Nibbles the Cat
landmark: Dumpster near V's apartment; cat food sourced from Arasaka Industrial Park
description: An adoptable cat. Grab the specific cat food from Arasaka Industrial Park first, then find the dumpster near the apartment (from the front door, turn left) and leave the food in the bowl.
requirements: Obtain cat food from Arasaka Industrial Park

name: Roach Race Arcade Game
landmark: Arcade cabinets scattered around Night City and in V's apartments
description: A playable arcade mini-game referencing a horse-racing side activity from CD Projekt Red's previous fantasy RPG.

name: Secret Phone Numbers
landmark: Dial from V's phone at any time
description: A handful of in-game phone numbers play hidden audio easter eggs when called, including musical references.

name: World's Best Surgeon
landmark: Afterlife bar, via NPC Dennis
description: Accepting the Big in Japan side quest from Dennis leads to a character clearly modeled on a well-known sitcom's oddball surgeon.
requirements: Accept the Big in Japan side quest

name: John Wick Gun Case
landmark: Weapons shop near the fast-travel point inside EBM Petrochem Stadium
description: A gun case addressed to John Wick sits at the back of the shop, with Johnny commenting on it.

name: Brendan the Vending Machine
landmark: Large shipping container in Japantown
description: A talkative vending machine NPC who asks you to clear his view before offering a follow-up quest later in the game.

name: CD Projekt Red Founders Cameo
landmark: Outside a clothing store near Petrochem, inside a derelict van
description: Two NPCs modeled on the real-life co-founders of CD Projekt Red sell junk items that reference the studio's other work.

name: Blood Lake
landmark: Rocky Ridge, north-northeast of the Desert Film Set Data Term
description: A disturbing lake of blood filled with mannequin bodies.

name: Rook
landmark: A cache machine below the main street, under the 'Aesculapius Inc.' sign, in an area of abandoned stores
description: A suppressed power pistol with high critical chance that builds with each successive hit.

name: Claw X-MOD2
landmark: Top of the pyramid-shaped building where Mr. Hands resides
description: A throwable melee axe with extra mod slots and improved handling. Requires repeated double-jumping to scale the building.

name: Genjiroh
landmark: Arasaka Estate, main lobby, on a desk through the door across from the stairs
description: A smart pistol firing four homing rounds per shot with electrical damage. Reach it by double-jumping over the estate's front wall using the bushes.

name: Lizzie
landmark: Lizzie's Bar basement, private room opposite Judy's braindance studio
description: A close-range tech pistol that behaves like a wall-piercing shotgun.
requirements: Available after the Automatic Love main job

name: Hawk
landmark: Leaning against a wall inside your Dogtown hideout on Kress Street
description: A power assault rifle that heavily debuffs enemies on hit. Doesn't auto-highlight, so use your scanner.
requirements: Complete the Lucretia My Reflection main job, then double back into the hideout instead of following Reed and Myers out

name: Ba Xing Chong
landmark: Adam Smasher's hideout aboard the Ebunike
description: A smart shotgun that fires homing, explosive rounds with a high dismemberment chance. Crafted from a schematic found in Smasher's vault.
requirements: Defeat Adam Smasher and take his access code, only obtainable near the very end of the game

name: Pozhar X-MOD2
landmark: An under-construction apartment building near Kress Street, during an early-morning house party
description: An improved rapid-fire power shotgun with guaranteed extra mod slots. Only accessible in the early hours before roughly 5 AM in-game.

name: ARCH Nazare 'Itsumade'
landmark: A garage in Northside Watson, next to an All Foods location
description: A fast, agile motorcycle sitting in a locked garage.

name: Quadra Type-66 'Hoon'
landmark: A near-empty warehouse at the north end of Watson
description: An armed nomad off-roader, effectively a gun-equipped version of the Type-66 Javelina. Keys are hidden in a nearby rubble pile.
requirements: Triggers the 'I'm in Love With My Car' side job; only available from the start of Act 2 onward

name: Amnesty
landmark: Won from Cassidy's bottle-shooting challenge near the Aldecaldos camp
description: A power revolver rewarded for beating a timed bottle-shooting challenge. No quest marker points to it directly.
requirements: Only available past the point of no return, after siding with Panam and the Aldecaldos in 'We Gotta Live Together'

name: Apparition
landmark: Looted from Frank Nostra's corpse, or purchased later from the Black Market dealer
description: A tech pistol obtained at the end of the Corpo-exclusive quest 'War Pigs'. Non-Corpo characters can buy it afterward from Dogtown's black market vendor.
requirements: Corpo lifepath quest, or purchase after completing it

name: Archangel
landmark: Given by Kerry after the reunion concert
description: A power revolver Kerry gives you after the reunion concert during 'A Like Supreme', near the end of Kerry and Johnny's questline.

name: BFC 9000
landmark: Crash-landed AV on the cliffs near Petrochem Dam, southern edge of Rancho Coronado
description: A novelty melee club dropped by an NPC at an AV crash site. The AV doesn't always appear to be present.

name: Black Unicorn
landmark: Appears directly in your storage
description: A katana rewarded for linking or creating a CD Projekt Red account through the game's launcher.

name: Bloody Maria
landmark: Sent to storage by El Capitan
description: A power shotgun rewarded for completing every gig in Santo Domingo.

name: Blue Fang
landmark: Melee weapons vendor's trailer, east of the Biotechnica protein farms
description: A purchasable iconic knife sold by a Badlands melee vendor.

name: Breakthrough
landmark: NCPD alert behind a house near the power substation, Rancho Coronado
description: A tech sniper rifle schematic looted from a miniboss.

name: Butcher's Cleaver
landmark: Butcher shop counter, Coastview
description: A knife grabbed from the counter during the mission 'M'ap Tann Pèlen'. The room becomes inaccessible once you leave.

name: Buzzsaw
landmark: NCPD alert behind a ripperdoc, Northside
description: A power SMG schematic looted from a miniboss.

name: Byakko
landmark: Sent to storage by Wakako
description: A katana rewarded for completing every gig in Westbrook.

name: Caretaker's Spade
landmark: Rainforest meeting room inside Arasaka Tower, off the path to the right
description: A hammer found during the final mission, referencing a boss from CD Projekt Red's previous fantasy RPG.
requirements: Past the point of no return, final mission only

name: Chaos
landmark: Royce's corpse at the All Foods Plant
description: A tech pistol looted from Royce during 'The Pickup'. Exact spot depends on how the encounter plays out.

name: Cocktail Stick
landmark: Vanity near Evelyn's jacket, VIP level of Clouds
description: A katana found only during the mission 'Automatic Love'.

name: Comrade's Hammer
landmark: NCPD alert near the Drop Point and Red Dirt Bar, Arroyo
description: A tech revolver schematic looted from a miniboss.

name: Cottonmouth
landmark: Table in Fingers' bedroom, Jig-Jig Street
description: A melee club found in a room only accessible during Judy's mission 'The Space In Between'.

name: Crash
landmark: Trailer park, given directly by River Ward
description: A power revolver River gives you at the end of his questline.

name: Death and Taxes
landmark: Maiko's office chair at Clouds
description: A power pistol accessible only during the mission 'Ex-Factor'.

name: Divided We Stand
landmark: 6th Street gang party near the ripperdoc, Rancho Coronado
description: A smart assault rifle obtainable by defeating or out-competing the 6th Street gang.

name: Dying Night
landmark: Wilson's weapon shop, Megabuilding H10
description: A power pistol purchasable from Wilson once he texts you it's ready, triggering the side job 'The Gun'.

name: Errata
landmark: Inside a furnace, Scav hideout from 'Disasterpiece'
description: A thermal katana found while rescuing Evelyn Parker with Judy. Area stays accessible afterward.

name: Fenrir
landmark: Medical table in a Maelstrom hideout near NID: Docks, Northside
description: A power SMG found while rescuing a monk's friend near the docks.

name: Gold-Plated Baseball Bat
landmark: Near a cement-filled pool, during 'Second Conflict'
description: A two-handed club set down by an NPC during a Kerry/Johnny questline mission.

name: Gwynbleidd
landmark: Appears directly in your storage
description: A sword rewarded for having The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt linked to the same CDPR account.

name: Headhunter
landmark: Melee weapon vendor, West Wind Estate
description: A purchasable iconic knife.

name: Hypercritical
landmark: Bathroom counter, Kabuki
description: A power precision rifle found while helping Tiny Mike during the gig 'Concrete Cage Trap'.

name: Jinchu-Maru
landmark: Looted from Oda after 'Play It Safe'
description: A katana obtainable whether or not Oda is killed during the encounter.

name: Kongou
landmark: Nightstand in Yorinobu's penthouse bedroom, Konpeki Plaza
description: A power pistol found during 'The Heist'.

name: La Chingona Dorada
landmark: Ofrenda at El Coyote Cojo, The Glen
description: A pair of power pistols left in tribute after Jackie's funeral, refunding ammo on headshots when swapping between them.

name: Lexington x-MOD2
landmark: Wilson's weapon shop, Megabuilding H10
description: A moddable power pistol rewarded for winning Wilson's shooting competition early in the game.

name: Malorian Arms 3516
landmark: Taken from Grayson aboard the Ebunike
description: A power pistol taken during the mission 'Chippin' In', part of Johnny's questline.

name: Moron Labe
landmark: NCPD alert near a warehouse, western West Wind Estate
description: A power assault rifle schematic looted from a miniboss named Anton Kolev.

name: Mox
landmark: Kitchen counter in Judy's apartment, Charter St
description: A power shotgun Judy leaves behind after her questline ends, if you remain on good terms with her.
requirements: Complete Judy's questline on good terms

name: Nehan
landmark: Saburo Arasaka's corpse, Konpeki Plaza
description: A knife looted during 'The Heist'.

name: O'Five
landmark: Won from Buck during 'Beat on the Brat: Arroyo'
description: A power sniper rifle wagered and won during a boxing side job.

name: Overwatch
landmark: Given by Panam after 'Riders on the Storm', Ingall's Farm
description: A power sniper rifle given the morning after spending the night at the farm.

name: Plan B
landmark: Dexter DeShawn's corpse at a trash heap
description: A power pistol looted where Takemura left Dexter's body.

name: Prejudice
landmark: Mortuary drawer at the Afterlife
description: A power assault rifle found during 'For Whom The Bell Tolls'.
requirements: Past the point of no return, Rogue/Johnny ending path

name: Pride
landmark: Rogue's remains, Arasaka Tower
description: A power pistol looted after defeating Adam Smasher during 'Knockin' on Heaven's Door'.

name: Problem Solver
landmark: Near the main entrance to the Wraith Camp
description: A power SMG looted from an ordinary Raffen Shiv enemy during 'Riders on the Storm'.

name: Prototype: Shingen Mark V
landmark: Shipping container outside a warehouse, Arasaka Industrial Park, Arroyo
description: A smart SMG found during 'Gimme Danger' with Takemura. Watch for a mine on the floor nearby.

name: Psalm 11:6
landmark: NCPD alert near the megabuilding and docks, Northside
description: A power assault rifle schematic looted from a miniboss.

name: Sasquatch's Hammer
landmark: Grand Imperial Mall, during the boss fight with Sasquatch
description: A hammer obtained during 'I Walk The Line', part of the Voodoo Boys questline.

name: Satori
landmark: Saburo Arasaka's personal AV atop Konpeki Plaza
description: A katana found during 'The Heist', reached the way Saburo enters the room.

name: Scalpel
landmark: A fridge inside a shack near the water, northern edge of Kabuki's shantytown
description: A katana rewarded for completing 'Big In Japan', started at the Afterlife once Rogue asks to be paid.

name: Scorch
landmark: Appears directly in your storage
description: A tech pistol rewarded for having Gwent: The Witcher Card Game linked to the same account.

name: Seraph
landmark: Sent to storage by Padre
description: A power pistol rewarded for completing every gig in Heywood.

name: Sir John Phallustiff
landmark: Meredith Stout's room at the No Tell Motel
description: A novelty melee club obtained during 'Venus in Furs', if you follow Meredith's instructions in 'The Pickup'.

name: Skippy
landmark: Alley in Vista Del Rey; can later be reset via fixer Regina Jones
description: A talking smart pistol tied to its own quest. Using it repeatedly progresses the storyline, and Regina can later reset it into a fixed, unmoddable form.

name: Sovereign
landmark: NCPD alert south of Jig-Jig Street, Japantown
description: A power double-barrel shotgun looted from a miniboss.

name: Stinger
landmark: Given by Mitch after 'I'll Fly Away'
description: A knife rewarded for helping fulfill Scorpion's last wish during Panam's questline.

name: The Headsman
landmark: Warehouse at the back of an NCPD alert site, North Oak
description: A power shotgun looted from a miniboss.

name: Tinker Bell
landmark: Security room at Edgewood Farms
description: A melee club found during River Ward's mission 'The Hunt'.

name: Tsumetogi
landmark: Screening room, Japantown
description: A katana looted after the violence concludes during 'Pisces', part of Judy's questline.

name: Widow Maker
landmark: A tunnel, during 'Ghost Town'
description: A tech precision rifle looted from Nash after tracking down his Raffen Shiv crew for Panam.

name: Yinglong
landmark: NCPD alert near the southwest edge of Wellsprings
description: A smart SMG schematic looted from an Animals gang miniboss.
